description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, both made of metallic material with a slightly weathered, matte finish. The blades are attached to a cylindrical, dark brown central head, which appears to be made of wood or a similarly dense material. The handle is long, straight, and metallic, featuring subtle vertical grooves for grip and a pointed end. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or engravings. The axe is proportioned for two-handed use, suggesting it is intended for heavy-duty chopping or splitting tasks. Its construction and materials indicate durability and effectiveness for both display and practical use.
description: A double-headed battle axe with broad metal blades, a dark cylindrical head, and a long, grooved metallic handle. The design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical metal blades and the cylindrical head with the full length of the handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical head, with the handle's grooves visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the cylindrical head, mirroring view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, metallic handle with vertical grooves for grip; cylindrical cross-section; appears rigid and ergonomic.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, broad metal blades with a slightly curved edge profile; no beard or poll; functional and undecorated.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted through the cylindrical head.
